items.title: Tobacco/Vape Industry’s Pivot To Religious Authorities A Public Health Concern — Fahmi Hassan
items.summary: The tobacco industry's meeting with a religious office is very alarming. Having faced firm resistance from health authorities, the industry is searching for new avenues of validation. Heated tobacco products and vapes remain harmful. The post Tobacco/Vape Industry’s Pivot To Religious Authorities A Public Health Concern — Fahmi Hassan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: After Meeting Philip Morris, FT Mufti Office Explores Fatwa For Shariah-Compliant Tobacco Alternatives
items.summary: The FT Mufti's Office says it's looking at the role of productive fatwas for "Shariah-compliant" alternatives to conventional cigarettes, after receiving a visit from Philip Morris Malaysia. Currently, smoking (including e-cigarettes) is considered haram. The post After Meeting Philip Morris, FT Mufti Office Explores Fatwa For Shariah-Compliant Tobacco Alternatives appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia’s Medicine Security At Risk Due To Persian Gulf Conflict
items.summary: Malaysia’s potential pharmaceutical vulnerability is exposed with alarming clarity. Malaysia is a net importer of pharmaceuticals. Generic medicines are often the most exposed to supply shocks. The government should establish a Health Security Agency. The post Malaysia’s Medicine Security At Risk Due To Persian Gulf Conflict appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysian Pharma Monitoring Potential Drug Supply Disruptions; Medical Devices, Supplies, Packaging At Risk Too
items.summary: Malaysian pharma suppliers are monitoring potential medicine supply disruptions from the Iran war. India, a major generics producer, comprises >30% of Malaysia's imported drug supply. Medical devices (MRI scanners), supplies, and packaging are at risk too. The post Malaysian Pharma Monitoring Potential Drug Supply Disruptions; Medical Devices, Supplies, Packaging At Risk Too appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Experts Warn Of Food Price Hikes In Malaysia From Fertiliser Cost Surge
items.summary: Fertiliser shocks from the Iran war may push up prices of vegetables, rice, chicken and eggs in Malaysia, as well as flour-based foods. Experts warn that lower yields, higher feed/fuel costs, and import reliance may worsen inflation if disruptions persist. The post Experts Warn Of Food Price Hikes In Malaysia From Fertiliser Cost Surge appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Greater Transparency Needed In Doctor Placement Policies — Family Medicine Specialist
items.summary: While many doctors remain deeply committed to serving wherever they are needed, policies governing placement, relocation, and support should be transparent, consistent, and responsive to the realities faced by health care professionals. The post Greater Transparency Needed In Doctor Placement Policies — Family Medicine Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia’s Public Health Care Is Flatlining — Public Health Medicine Specialist
items.summary: Malaysia's public health care is flatlining; PR stunts won't save it. Our public health care cannot depend indefinitely on doctors absorbing more pressure, more risk, and more disappointment while being told to be grateful for symbolic gestures. The post Malaysia’s Public Health Care Is Flatlining — Public Health Medicine Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Forward Buying, Strong Feed Supply Buffer Malaysia’s Food Costs For Now: Importer
items.summary: Malaysian livestock producers have secured feed supply months in advance, while global corn and soybean stocks remain high, limiting immediate cost pressures despite rising diesel prices, says a commodities importer. The post Forward Buying, Strong Feed Supply Buffer Malaysia’s Food Costs For Now: Importer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: SSB Tax Fails To Cut Sugary Drinks Consumption: ISIS Malaysia
items.summary: ISIS Malaysia says the SSB tax, a volumetric tax system, is largely ineffective in reducing sugary drinks consumption, with a 10% increase in SSB tax reducing purchases by only less than 2%. ISIS also recommends expanding the SSB tax to condensed milk. The post SSB Tax Fails To Cut Sugary Drinks Consumption: ISIS Malaysia appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Who Protects The Consumer In Private Hospitals? — Ravinder Singh
items.summary: If private hospital costs continue to rise without stronger safeguards for consumers, public trust will inevitably be tested. The post Who Protects The Consumer In Private Hospitals? — Ravinder Singh appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ex-senior manager claims trial to two counts of receiving RM2.1m in stolen funds in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, March 19 — A former senior manager of a multinational company claimed trial to two counts of dishonestl...
